title: Xtra Crunchy Wide Italian Pasta Flatbread
description: Winey, crunchy, hangover-cure Italian Piedmontese dish featuring vanilla, beurre-monte, lacto-vegetarian-friendly, form=piadina, vessel=cazuela.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the 00 Caputo Flour and a pinch of salt. Mix until just combined, then cover and let rest at room temperature for 1 hour.
After the dough has rested, divide it into 2 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Using a rolling pin, roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium-high heat. Cook the pasta flatbread for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and crispy.
Transfer the cooked pasta flatbread to a plate and top with the Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese, Mascarpone cheese, and a sprinkle of salt.
In a small bowl, mix together the softened butter and vanilla extract to make the beurre-monte. Spoon the beurre-monte over the top of the flatbread.
Drizzle the lemon zest over the top of the flatbread and garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
Cut the flatbread into wedges and serve immediately.
To make the Beurre Monte, melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a small saucepan over low heat. Whisk in 2 tablespoons of whole milk and continue to cook until the mixture thickens to your liking.
Season the Beurre Monte with salt and pepper to taste, then spoon it over the top of the pasta flatbread.
Serve the Xtra Crunchy Wide Italian Pasta Flatbread hot, garnished with additional Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese and fresh parsley, if desired.
